question_text: A motor lifts a 50 kg mass 8 metres in 20 seconds. The motor's power input is 250 W. Calculate the efficiency of the motor. (Use g = 10 m/s²)
explanation: Work output = mgh = 50 × 10 × 8 = 4000 J. Power output = Work ÷ Time = 4000 ÷ 20 = 200 W. Efficiency = (Power output ÷ Power input) × 100% = (200 ÷ 250) × 100% = 80%.
